content.text:
[cron:finance-email-processor Finance & Invoice Email Processor] Process financial emails from Gmail labels for Alfred. IMPORTANT Himalaya CLI syntax — flags BEFORE query.

## Step 1: Fetch unprocessed emails from financial labels
Scan these Gmail label folders for emails that do NOT have the "flagged" flag:

1. himalaya envelope list -f "Finances" -o json "not flag flagged"
2. himalaya envelope list -f "Invoices" -o json "not flag flagged"
3. himalaya envelope list -f "Finances/TAX" -o json "not flag flagged"
4. himalaya envelope list -f "Finances/Investments" -o json "not flag flagged"

Note: himalaya outputs a WARN line before the JSON array. Ignore it.

## Step 2: Read and extract from each email
For each unprocessed email:
- Read body: himalaya message read <ID> -f "<FOLDER>" --preview --no-headers -o plain
- IMPORTANT: use the correct -f folder flag matching where you found the email

## Step 3: Create structured markdown for Alfred
For each email, create a file with PROPER YAML FRONTMATTER (ALL string values MUST be quoted):

```markdown
---
alfred_tags:
- finance/processed
date: YYYY-MM-DD
source: "<sender name or company>"
source_email: <sender@email.com>
subject: "<Full Subject Line - ALWAYS quote this>"
type: finance
category: <invoice|tax|investment|insurance|property|subscription|receipt>
amount: "<amount if found, e.g. EUR 29.99>"
---

# <Subject>

**From:** <sender>
**Date:** <date>
**Category:** <invoice|tax|investment|insurance|property|subscription|receipt>
**Amount:** <if identifiable>
**Tax-relevant:** <yes|no>

## Summary
<2-3 sentence summary>

## Key Details
- <bullet points with amounts, dates, account numbers, reference numbers>

## Action Items
<any follow-ups needed: payment due, response required, filing needed>
```

## Step 4: Save to Alfred inbox
- Filename: `finance-<category>-<YYYYMMDD>-<safe-subject-slug>.md`
- Save to: `/home/trader/.openclaw/workspace/inbox/`
- Alfred Curator will pick these up automatically

## Step 5: Mark as processed
For each processed email, add the flagged flag:
- himalaya flag set -f "<FOLDER>" <ID> flagged
- Do NOT move or delete the email — leave it in its label folder
- Do NOT mark as unseen

## Guidelines
- Process maximum 20 emails per run (oldest first)
- Preserve ALL financial details: amounts, dates, reference/invoice numbers, account details
- Flag tax-relevant items clearly
- For German emails (Rechnung, Honorarnote, etc.), translate key terms but keep original reference numbers
- Skip obvious duplicates (same invoice appearing in multiple labels)
- If an email has no financial content (e.g. marketing from a financial service), skip it and still mark as flagged

## Error handling
- If a folder returns empty or errors, skip it and continue with the next
- If himalaya fails to read a message, skip it and continue
- Log any skipped emails at the end
Current time: Saturday, March 21st, 2026 — 11:00 AM (UTC) / 2026-03-21 11:00 UTC

Return your summary as plain text; it will be delivered automatically. If the task explicitly calls for messaging a specific external recipient, note who/where it should go instead of sending it yourself.

Dear Investors,

We hope everyone is having a wonderful holiday season! November was another solid month for Rent Ready, with $763k of gross revenue up 114% over last year. Net revenue was $140k up 79% over last year. We also delivered our first flooring installation service as part of our beta testing. This is a huge milestone for the business and represents a major growth opportunity going forward. The rest of this update provides further details on these key areas:


  *   Performance update
  *   New markets update
  *   Product update

Performance Update

[cid:image001.png@01D91B8A.53700F70]
We saw the impact of seasonality in November, which resulted in a month over month decline, which we expect to reverse by January as we grow through the impact of seasonality.


[cid:image002.png@01D91B8A.53700F70]


  *   November gross revenue was up 114% year over year and 5% behind forecast
  *   Net revenue was $140k up 79% year over year and 8% behind forecast
  *   Net margin was 18% vs. forecast 19%
  *   Burn was ($527k), above forecasted burn of ($509k)


New Markets Update

New markets were down in November vs. October due to seasonality with $95k of gross revenue.

[cid:image003.png@01D91B8A.53700F70]


Product Update

We have completed the initial buildout of our Provider application. As our strategy shifted to platform this year and we began partnering with providers that had multiple crews instead of just individuals the requirements and value that we could add to their business management increased significantly. Almost all providers in the multifamily space operate on pen and paper with no software to manage critical business functions. This makes running and scaling their businesses chaotic and overwhelming. Our provider app will serve as a business management solution that allows providers to manage all aspects of their work with Rent Ready including receiving work, setting up crews, managing capacity, viewing payments, scheduling, and tracking/monitoring work completion in real time. This is the foundation for bringing the same level of world class product that we brought to clients to our providers and will help us attract and retain the best providers in the market.



We are very excited about the continued progress we're making.

Thank you for your support,
The Rent Ready Team


Will Brugh
Co-founder
Rent Ready

[cid:image004.png@01D91B8A.53700F70]

[cid:image001.png@01D91B8A.53700F70]
We saw the impact of seasonality in November, which resulted in a month over month decline, which we expect to reverse by January as we grow through the impact of seasonality.


[cid:image002.png@01D91B8A.53700F70]


  *   November gross revenue was up 114% year over year and 5% behind forecast
  *   Net revenue was $140k up 79% year over year and 8% behind forecast
  *   Net margin was 18% vs. forecast 19%
  *   Burn was ($527k), above forecasted burn of ($509k)


New Markets Update

New markets were down in November vs. October due to seasonality with $95k of gross revenue.

[cid:image003.png@01D91B8A.53700F70]


Product Update

We have completed the initial buildout of our Provider application. As our strategy shifted to platform this year and we began partnering with providers that had multiple crews instead of just individuals the requirements and value that we could add to their business management increased significantly. Almost all providers in the multifamily space operate on pen and paper with no software to manage critical business functions. This makes running and scaling their businesses chaotic and overwhelming. Our provider app will serve as a business management solution that allows providers to manage all aspects of their work with Rent Ready including receiving work, setting up crews, managing capacity, viewing payments, scheduling, and tracking/monitoring work completion in real time. This is the foundation for bringing the same level of world class product that we brought to clients to our providers and will help us attract and retain the best providers in the market.



We are very excited about the continued progress we're making.

Thank you for your support,
The Rent Ready Team


Will Brugh
Co-founder
Rent Ready

content.text:
Dear Investors,

Rent Ready had an awesome March and Q1. We were up 120% year over year on gross revenue and 140% on net revenue in both the month and quarter. We significantly outperformed budget and were ahead by 17% on net revenue for the quarter and delivered burn of nearly $70k less than budget in March. We are very appreciative of the strong support we've experienced on the capital raise and have hit our $2.5mm target. As part of the raise, we landed Ginkgo Residential, an owner operator of roughly 8k units, as both an investor and client. They will start with a 1,600 unit pilot and have intentions to roll out portfolio wide upon continued success of the partnership. Fully rolled out Ginkgo represents a $1mm annual net revenue opportunity. It's a big win and will provide additional industry connectivity and reference for other large deals. We will now look to tack on another $1.5-2.5mm to the round, which would provide the necessary capital to get to breakeven and create greater optionality on the timing of the Series B. The rest of this update provides further details on these key areas:


  *   Performance update
  *   New markets update

OpenRouter Logo

Important Billing Update

------------------------
Important Billing Update
------------------------

Hi,

Starting Friday, March 6, we will begin collecting Value Added Tax (VAT) and any other applicable sales taxes for customers whose billing address is in the European Union.

Applicable VAT or sales tax will be automatically calculated and added to credit purchases. This change also applies to any configured Auto Top Up purchases. Taxes will be itemized on invoices and receipts.

If you're purchasing on behalf of a business, you can add a valid EU VAT ID or tax ID to your billing details to avoid being charged VAT.

To update your billing information or add your VAT ID:

* Go to your Credits dashboard ( https://openrouter.ai/credits?utm_campaign=EU+Sales+Tax&utm_content=EU+Sales+Tax&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )
* Click on History to open your billing portal
* Select Update information and add your VAT ID there

For details, you can view all invoices and payment history anytime in your
Credits dashboard ( https://openrouter.ai/credits?utm_campaign=EU+Sales+Tax&utm_content=EU+Sales+Tax&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ).

If you believe your tax treatment was incorrect, please reach out to us with relevant documentation at
support@openrouter.ai ( support@openrouter.ai?utm_campaign=EU+Sales+Tax&utm_content=EU+Sales+Tax&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ).

Thanks for using OpenRouter!

The OpenRouter Team
